text:
A boy named Sam lost his glasses the other day. He looked around desperately, but couldn't find them anywhere. His afternoon routine was ruined. Desperate, he looked under the couch, between the cushions, and even down the rabbit hole. No luck. Finally, he remembered putting them in his lunch box earlier. He popped it open and saw the glasses gleaming on a paper clip.

He picked up the box and said goodbye to his afternoon plans. Instead, he went outside for a walk. It was nice to see the world through his eyes. He found a hawk perched on a branch, gazing down at a vole. The hawk let out a low, contented chuckle.

Sam chuckled, too. It was funny how looking through glasses can remind you so much about finding birds of prey inside small critters. He wound down his street, counting trees; they were getting less leafy.
